qa_pairs.question: How are jobs with longer run times handled on general-purpose clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: On general-purpose clusters, jobs with longer run times are restricted to using only a fraction of the cluster's resources, which is managed through 'partitions'.

qa_pairs.question: What are the various time-based partitions available for jobs on general-purpose clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: General-purpose clusters provide partitions for jobs with maximum run times of 3 hours or less, 12 hours or less, 24 hours (1 day) or less, 72 hours (3 days) or less, and 7 days or less.

qa_pairs.question: How does the requested time limit of a job affect its scheduling opportunities on general-purpose clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: A shorter job will have more scheduling opportunities than an otherwise-identical longer job because it can run in its own time-limit partition as well as in partitions designed for longer time limits.
